title: Hook Contract Design
prompt: Design two custom hooks for a plugin that imports external records: one action after a record is imported and one filter before a record is saved. Name each hook, describe timing, payload shape, and compatibility promises.
hints: Actions announce events; filters transform values and must return the filtered value.

model_answer.summary: Custom hooks are public contracts, so their names, timing, payload, and compatibility promises need to be stable and documented.
model_answer.work: Define an action such as my_plugin_record_imported that fires after a record has been validated, saved, and assigned an internal ID. Its arguments can be $record_id, $source_record, and $context, with the payload documented as arrays containing stable keys. Define a filter such as my_plugin_pre_save_record that runs after normalization but before database write; it receives $record, $source_record, and $context and must return the filtered record. Use a plugin slug or namespace-style prefix to avoid collisions, document the hook version, and do not break argument order or return expectations without a deprecation path.
checks: Verify the action fires only after a successful import.
Test that the filter return value is used and validated before save.
Document hook names, timing, arguments, payload keys, and return expectations.
Review names for prefix or namespace uniqueness.
Add compatibility notes before changing arguments in a future version.

rubric_mapping.criterion: Uses namespaced or prefixed hook names

model_answer.why_this_is_good: It teaches that hooks are not just extension points; they are APIs with compatibility obligations.
common_misses: Using a filter but forgetting to return the modified value.
Firing the action before the import has actually succeeded.
Choosing generic hook names that can collide.
Changing payload shape without documenting compatibility.
model_answer.revision_prompt: Revise by naming one action and one filter, then specify timing, arguments, payload shape, return value, prefixing, and compatibility promises.

criteria.name: Uses namespaced or prefixed hook names
